source: MBC Overland Travel + Mörk Borg Bare Bones Edition. Off-road entry 9 is absent from the source (hidden behind the page illustration — confirmed by OCR); the entry here is homebrew filler so the d12 stays rollable.
road_types: Narrow wagon track used by farmers.
Tracks drawn up by wild beasts or savage monsters.
Once well-maintained trade road, now fallen into disrepair.
Once well-maintained trade road, now fallen into disrepair.
Paved road, sporadically patrolled by the King's soldiers.
events: Nothing particular happens. The world is grey.
Nothing particular happens. The world is grey.
Nothing particular happens. The world is grey.
The weather turns worse. You make no progress today.
Weather change. Reroll on the weather table.
Weather change. Reroll on the weather table.
The road forks. The signs are unintelligible. Reroll the road type.
The road forks. The signs are unintelligible. Reroll the road type.
1d6 rations of food or water become spoiled.
A monastery stands by the road. Chanting inside — Nechrubel cultists.
Ruins of a castle. Infested with savage crows; a blind alchemist lives in the lone tower.
A religious procession of flagellants and hermits, lost on the way to seeing HIM.
A funerary procession with a very large coffin. The giant inside is dead but dreaming.
Filthy farmers on their way to the market.
1d6+1 slavers, leading 2d6 slaves; half beaten to death.
A few mercenaries and their 1d8 guards — all infected with a brain parasite.
Battle across the road between flayed cultists and a tribe of Earthbound (Outcasts).
The troll Adnah (or Arbint) ambushes you.
Abandoned graveyard. Inverted gold cross (50s) in a chapel; d8 zombies in the crypt.
Two dead bodies by the side of the road.
navigation: You get lost and spend all day before finding the road again.
You find d6+1 rations, but one is tainted (Pre dr12 to notice).
You find d6+3 rations of good food or fresh water.
You track and kill a savage beast — d8+2 rations of meat.
A village. Roll on the village table to see what you find.
A village. Roll on the village table to see what you find.
villages: Deserted; d6 rations can be scavenged from the ruins.
Stricken by plague; any ration is 2-in-6 tainted.
Well-off trading post. Food and water plentiful and cheap.
Ruled by bandits. Don't show off your wealth.
Dominated by spider cultists. No trading with outsiders.
Inhabited by cannibals. No water for sale but plenty of food.
off_road: An ancient battlefield, full of half-buried rusty blades and wraiths.
A valley full of the bones of extinct animals.
A temple to a pre-human god, covered in lichen.
An entrance to the deeper underworld.
A ruined abbey. Guarded by a Grotesque; an occult artifact hidden in the crypt.
A black mire, sucking and silent.
The silhouette of a Wyvern against the purple evening sky.
A clock tower, chiming. Heretic witches make unliving troll hybrids in its shadow.
A vast bog. Ruled by a huge, bloated, tentacular thing that claims to have been royalty.
